design.ml_concept: In-Context Learning — the ability of large language models to learn new tasks from examples provided in the prompt without weight updates. Objects represent the components of few-shot learning: demonstrations, pattern matching, format recognition, and chain-of-thought reasoning.
design.description: A grand hall lined with demonstration easels, each displaying worked examples that glow when studied. The air shimmers with latent patterns — study one example, and others like it brighten; study enough, and a new understanding crystallizes. At the far end, a chalkboard shows step-by-step reasoning chains connecting inputs to outputs. No weights are changed here — only the context window expands with insight. The hall teaches that sometimes the most powerful learning requires no learning at all.
